format: Return a single JSON object with the required top-level keys. Do not include markdown or explanatory prose.

queue_items.ordering: Sort by route_priority ascending, with priority 1 first.

route_priority.description: Use 1 for the highest priority item. Values must be unique across returned queue items.

deadline.description: Use the appeal deadline date when applicable; otherwise use an empty string.

reason.description: Use the controlled reason for an MD escalation item; otherwise use none.

recovery_amount.description: Use the recovery amount for a claim correction item; otherwise use 0.00.

aggregate_counts.description: Count returned queue items by final route.

total_recovery_amount.description: Sum of recovery amounts across all routed queue items.

basis_audit.description: Business basis audit trail. Populate it using the source-precedence and record-grounding convention inferred from solved examples; use environment record IDs and business gap keys, not invented control codes.

source_precedence.description: Use the concise source-precedence category convention inferred from solved examples.

controlling_record_ids.ordering_rule: Use the operational evidence order for the records that directly control the result.

exception_record_ids.ordering_rule: Use business gap/exception order: criteria or route gaps before stale or excluded records when both appear.

precedence_record_order.ordering_rule: List the controlling and exception records in source-precedence order, highest priority first.
